LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, MAY-27-2021 HEARING HELD RE: DEFENDANTS JAMES SMITH, KINDRED HEALTHCARE OPERATING, INC., HILLHAVEN-MSC PARTNERSHIP, YGNACIO VALLEY CARE CENTER LLC, AND KINDRED NURSING CENTERS WEST, LLC'S MOTION TO DISMISS FOR IMPROPER VENUE PURSUANT TO CALIFORNIA CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTIONS 410.30 AND 418.10, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO DISMISS FOR IMPROPER VENUE IS GRANTED IN PART. THE COURT DENIES DISMISSAL BUT STAYS THIS ACTION BASED ON THE ENFORCEABLE FORUM SELECTION CLAUSE. THE COURT STRIKES THE PARAGRAPH IN DEFENDANTS' PROPOSED ORDER WHICH WAS NOT PART OF THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING, AND SIGNS A FORMAL ORDER. (SEE SIGNED ORDER FOR COMPLETE RULING.) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: ROSE MILLER, CSR NO. 5041, EMAIL: RMILLERCSR@GMAIL.COM (302/EPS)

THE VERIFIED APPLICATION OF RYAN MCCARTY TO APPEAR AS COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE FOR DEFENDANTS MEETS THE STANDARDS PRESCRIBED BY CAL. R. CT. 9.40. THE COURT NOTES THAT THE APPLICATION SHOULD HAVE BEEN FILED UPON STATUTORY NOTICE (CAL. R. CT. 9.40(C)(1),) BUT GRANTS THE APPLICATION AT THE HEARING AS NOT OPPOSED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Improper Venue Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 410 30 And 418 10
Off Calendar May-27-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. TODD W. SMITH, Esq. of Umberg Zipser LLP (Tel: 949-679-0052) for Plaintiffs ASJK, LLC, AWCY, LLC, and ASFP, LLC.
Attys. RYAN MCCARTY, Esq., TERI H.P. NGUYEN, Esq., and MELENIE VAN, Esq. of Polsinelli LLP (Tel: 415-248-2100) for Defendants Kindred Healthcare Operating, Inc., Hillhaven-MSC Partnership, Ygnacio Valley Care Center LLC, Kindred Nursing Centers West, LLC, and James Smith.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS JAMES SMITH, KINDRED HEALTHCARE OPERATING, INC., HILLHAVEN-MSC PARTNERSHIP, YGNACIO VALLEY CARE CENTER LLC, AND KINDRED NURSING CENTERS WEST, LLC's Motion To Dismiss For Improper Venue Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 410.30 And 418.10.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' motion to dismiss for improper venue is granted in part. The Court denies dismissal but stays this action based on the enforceable forum selection clause.

(See Word minutes for complete ruling.)

The Court strikes the paragraph in defendants' proposed order which was not part of the Court's tentative ruling, and signs a formal order.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com (302/EPS)

Notice Of Unopposed Motion To File Under Seal Exhibits 1 2 3 And 4 To The Motion To Dismiss For Improper Venue Of Defendants Kindred Healthcare Operating Inc Hillhaven Msc Partnership Ygnacio Valley Care Center Llc Kindred Nursing Centers West Llc And James Smith
Off Calendar May-27-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. TODD W. SMITH, Esq. of Umberg Zipser LLP (Tel: 949-679-0052) for Plaintiffs ASJK, LLC, AWCY, LLC, and ASFP, LLC.
Attys. RYAN MCCARTY, Esq., TERI H.P. NGUYEN, Esq., and MELENIE VAN, Esq. of Polsinelli LLP (Tel: 415-248-2100) for Defendants Kindred Healthcare Operating, Inc., Hillhaven-MSC Partnership, Ygnacio Valley Care Center LLC, Kindred Nursing Centers West, LLC, and James Smith.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS JAMES SMITH, KINDRED HEALTHCARE OPERATING, INC., HILLHAVEN-MSC PARTNERSHIP, YGNACIO VALLEY CARE CENTER LLC, AND KINDRED NURSING CENTERS WEST, LLC's Unopposed Motion To File Under Seal Exhibits 1 2 3 And 4 To The Motion To Dismiss For Improper Venue Of Defendants Kindred Healthcare Operating Inc Hillhaven MSC Partnership Ygnacio Valley Care Center LLC Kindred Nursing Centers West LLC And James Smith.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' unopposed motion to file under seal Exhibits 1-4 to the motion to dismiss for improper venue is granted. No opposition filed and the motion complies with California Rules of Court, Rules 2.550 and 2.551. The Court finds that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the portions of the records to be sealed; the overriding interest supports sealing those records; a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com (302/EPS)

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar May-27-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. TODD W. SMITH, Esq. of Umberg Zipser LLP (Tel: 949-679-0052) for Plaintiffs ASJK, LLC, AWCY, LLC, and ASFP, LLC.
Attys. RYAN MCCARTY, Esq., TERI H.P. NGUYEN, Esq., and MELENIE VAN, Esq. of Polsinelli LLP (Tel: 415-248-2100) for Defendants Kindred Healthcare Operating, Inc., Hillhaven-MSC Partnership, Ygnacio Valley Care Center LLC, Kindred Nursing Centers West, LLC, and James Smith.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS JAMES SMITH, KINDRED HEALTHCARE OPERATING, INC., HILLHAVEN-MSC PARTNERSHIP, YGNACIO VALLEY CARE CENTER LLC, AND KINDRED NURSING CENTERS WEST, LLC'S MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The verified application of Ryan McCarty to appear as counsel pro hac vice for defendants meets the standards prescribed by Cal. R. Ct. 9.40. The Court notes that the application should have been filed upon statutory notice (Cal. R. Ct. 9.40(c)(1),) but it will grant the application at the hearing if it is not opposed.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com (302/EPS)
